# Copilot instructions for VisionAid (aura_app)

This file gives concise, actionable guidance for AI coding agents working on this Flutter app.

1) Big-picture architecture
- App entry: [lib/main.dart](lib/main.dart#L1-L400) — initializes Firebase, sets up `MultiProvider`, and configures `GoRouter` routes.
- State: simple `ChangeNotifier` providers in `lib/providers/` (e.g. `AuthProvider`, `AppProvider`, `OcrProvider`). Providers are created in `MyApp` and some expose `init()` methods (call patterns seen in `main.dart`).
- UI: screens live under `lib/screens/`; navigation is router-driven (paths like `/home`, `/live-vision`, `/ocr-reader`). Custom page transition defined as `FadeSlideTransition` in `main.dart`.
- ML & models: object-detection uses `ultralytics_yolo` and `tflite_flutter`; bundled model at `assets/models/yolov8s_float32.tflite` (ensure `flutter pub get` picks it up and `pubspec.yaml` assets include `assets/models/`).
- Firebase: initialized via `lib/firebase_options.dart` (generated by FlutterFire CLI). Android has `android/app/google-services.json` present; keep platform config in sync.

2) Critical developer workflows (commands you can run)
- Install deps: `flutter pub get`
- Run on device/emulator: `flutter run -d <device>` or use the IDE launch configuration.
- Build APK (release): `flutter build apk --release`
- Run widget tests: `flutter test test/widget_test.dart`
- Regenerate Firebase config: run `flutterfire configure` to regenerate `lib/firebase_options.dart` (the file currently contains generated placeholders).

3) Project-specific conventions & patterns
- Providers: always add new app-wide providers in `MyApp` `MultiProvider` (see [lib/main.dart](lib/main.dart#L40-L120)). If a provider requires async init, mirror existing pattern (`..init()` after constructor).
- Routing: use `GoRouter` routes defined in `main.dart`. Add new pages with a `GoRoute(path: '/x', pageBuilder: ...)` using the existing `FadeSlideTransition` wrapper for consistency.
- Assets: keep assets under `assets/images/`, `assets/icons/`, `assets/models/` and ensure `pubspec.yaml` includes those directories (see `pubspec.yaml` assets section).
- Native config: Android Firebase settings live in `android/app/google-services.json` and Gradle files under `android/` (this repo uses Kotlin Gradle scripts `.kts`). When changing Firebase, update both `google-services.json` and re-run `flutterfire configure`.

4) Integration points to watch when editing code
- Firebase (Auth / Firestore / Storage): `firebase_auth`, `cloud_firestore`, `firebase_storage`. Changes to auth flows affect `lib/providers/auth_provider.dart` and screens under `lib/screens/`.
- ML kits: `google_mlkit_text_recognition`, `google_mlkit_object_detection`, `ultralytics_yolo`, `tflite_flutter`. Keep model file names and asset paths in sync; large model changes may require updating Android/iOS packaging and increasing app size allowances.
- TTS + OCR: `flutter_tts` and `google_mlkit_text_recognition` used by `OcrProvider` — check `initTts()` patterns in provider code.

5) Safety, secrets, and generated files
- `lib/firebase_options.dart` is generated—do not hand-edit unless necessary. Instead, run `flutterfire configure` to re-generate.
- `android/app/google-services.json` is platform config and must match the Firebase project. Avoid accidental replacement with wrong project keys.

6) Small examples (copy-paste patterns)
- Registering a provider (follow existing style):

  ChangeNotifierProvider(create: (_) => NewProvider()..init()),

- Adding a route (follow `main.dart` pattern):

  GoRoute(path: '/my-page', pageBuilder: (c, s) => FadeSlideTransition(key: s.pageKey, child: const MyPage())),

7) Where to look first when debugging or extending
- App startup and routing: [lib/main.dart](lib/main.dart#L1-L400)
- Provider implementations: `lib/providers/`
- UI screens: `lib/screens/`
- Models & assets: `assets/models/` and `pubspec.yaml`
- Firebase configuration: `lib/firebase_options.dart` and `android/app/google-services.json`

If anything above is unclear or you'd like more examples (e.g., how to add a new ML model, update routing, or test providers), tell me which area to expand and I will update this file.
